messages.content:
I'll start by loading the execution-trace skill first, as required.

Now loading the report-analyzer skill and the document simultaneously.





Now I will analyze the lab report PDF and produce the 8 BloomCell-compliant JSON objects. Based on the document retrieved from the provided URL (`Document_2.pdf`) — a functional laboratory evaluation report — I apply the BloomCell 4-step analysis framework:

---

**Skill Output — 8 JSON Objects (BloomCell Analysis):**

```json
[
  {
    "id": "f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f78-9a0b-c1d2e3f4a5b6",
    "date": "06/05/26 11:13 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy 30-100 ng/mL 18.4",
    "group": "1. Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D is critically below BloomCell functional minimum (30–100 ng/mL) at 18.4 ng/mL, indicating moderate-to-severe deficiency with significant immune, hormonal, and musculoskeletal implications.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 55, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4089-b1c2-d3e4f5a6b7c8",
    "date": "06/05/26 11:13 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Vitamin D 18.4 ng/mL — critically below functional range; deficiency pattern with immune suppression and hormonal dysregulation risk.",
    "group": "1. Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D at 18.4 ng/mL is substantially below the BloomCell functional floor of 30 ng/mL (severity score 4/5). This level is associated with impaired innate immune response, increased inflammatory cytokine activity, reduced calcium absorption, and HPA-axis dysregulation. Cross-reference with PTH, calcium, magnesium, and CRP. Recommend confirming with 25-OH D3 specific assay. Consider loading dose protocol under physician supervision. Investigate malabsorption as contributing factor if supplementation history is present.",
    "referencesource": "3 M Health Check.docx - Page 2 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Row 55",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4190-c2d3-e4f5a6b7c8d9",
    "date": "06/05/26 11:13 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Hemoglobin A1c 4.8-5.6 % 6.1",
    "group": "2. Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "HbA1c is above BloomCell functional ceiling (4.8–5.6%) at 6.1%, indicating pre-diabetic blood sugar dysregulation that affects energy, cognition, and long-term metabolic health.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 12, Columns Maximum/High",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "c4d5e6f7-a8b9-4201-d3e4-f5a6b7c8d9e0",
    "date": "06/05/26 11:13 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Your blood sugar average (HbA1c) is slightly elevated. This can cause energy crashes, brain fog, and increased fatigue — but it is reversible with the right steps.",
    "group": "2. Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "HbA1c of 6.1% reflects 3-month average blood glucose in the pre-diabetic range. Elevated HbA1c impairs mitochondrial function, promotes glycation of proteins, and increases inflammatory load. Patients typically experience afternoon energy dips, difficulty concentrating, and disrupted sleep. This is a snapshot in time — dietary changes, exercise, and targeted supplementation can reverse pre-diabetic trends within 3–6 months.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Narrative results.docx - Page 1 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Row 12",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4312-e4f5-a6b7c8d9e0f1",
    "date": "06/05/26 11:13 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "hsCRP <1.0 mg/L 3.8",
    "group": "3. Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "hsCRP is 3.8× above the BloomCell optimal ceiling (<1.0 mg/L), indicating active systemic inflammation requiring immediate dietary and lifestyle intervention.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 22, Columns Maximum/Severity Score/Impact Score",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "e6f7a8b9-c0d1-4423-f5a6-b7c8d9e0f1a2",
    "date": "06/05/26 11:13 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Start Omega-3 (2g EPA+DHA daily). Eliminate refined sugar and seed oils. Begin 30-min daily walks. Retest hsCRP in 6 weeks.",
    "group": "3. Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "hsCRP at 3.8 mg/L exceeds the cardiovascular risk threshold and BloomCell functional limit (<1.0 mg/L). Omega-3 supplementation at 2g EPA+DHA daily has evidence-based anti-inflammatory effects within 4–6 weeks. Eliminating refined carbohydrates and seed oils reduces arachidonic acid-driven prostaglandin activity. Daily moderate-intensity exercise (30 min) lowers CRP independently of weight loss. Retesting at 6 weeks provides early intervention confirmation.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Recovery MVP v2.xlsx - Recovery Strategies sheet - Column 15 | BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Row 34",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "f7a8b9c0-d1e2-4534-a6b7-c8d9e0f1a2b3",
    "date": "06/05/26 11:13 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Ferritin 30-150 ng/mL 12.6",
    "group": "4. Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Ferritin is below BloomCell functional minimum (30–150 ng/mL) at 12.6 ng/mL, indicating depleted iron stores that chronically impair oxygen transport, energy metabolism, and thyroid function.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 15, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  },
  {
    "id": "a8b9c0d1-e2f3-4645-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c4",
    "date": "06/05/26 11:13 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Target Ferritin >50, Vitamin D >50, HbA1c <5.6, hsCRP <1.0. Retest: 6–8wks (Vit D, ferritin), 3mo (HbA1c, CRP), 6mo (full panel).",
    "group": "4. Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Low ferritin (12.6 ng/mL) combined with low Vitamin D and elevated HbA1c creates a compounding pattern of metabolic and immune vulnerability. Long-term strategy must address iron repletion (food-first, then supplemental bisglycinate if tolerated), sustained Vitamin D optimization to >50 ng/mL, and glycaemic control through dietary reform. Retest ferritin and Vitamin D at 6–8 weeks to assess repletion trajectory, HbA1c and CRP at 3 months, and full panel at 6 months. Consider GI-MAP to rule out malabsorption if ferritin and Vitamin D do not respond to supplementation.",
    "referencesource": "Master Report April 2026 (2).docx - Page 5 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - Row 11",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  }
]
```





Now saving all 8 merged records to Cosmos DB individually.



8 records saved successfully.
